statusLine: European fuel security is under strain with 17 of 27 countries below mandatory thresholds in at least one fuel type, though overall reserves remain above minimum requirements.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure despite adequate overall reserve levels. While EU averages of 98.7 days for petrol, 97.4 days for diesel, and 91.1 days for jet fuel exceed the mandatory 90-day minimum, the distribution across member states reveals concerning vulnerabilities. Romania shows the most severe shortfall with only 43.6 days of petrol reserves, well below the critical threshold.

The data reveals widespread deficiencies particularly in Eastern European nations. Poland, Romania, and Lithuania show critical shortages across multiple fuel types, while major economies like Germany, France, and Italy also face critical petrol shortages. Notably, 17 of 27 member states fall below mandatory thresholds in at least one fuel category, indicating systemic strain on European fuel security infrastructure.

Fuel prices reflect ongoing market pressures, with EU averages of €1.784 per liter for petrol and €2.021 per liter for diesel. The 10.77% decline in Brent crude to $88.69 per barrel provides some relief from recent highs, though prices remain elevated following the 27% increase since Middle East tensions began. The price differential between cheapest (Malta at €1.34) and most expensive (Netherlands at €2.316) markets shows significant regional variation in fuel costs across the union.
keyPoints: 17 of 27 EU countries have critical or warning-level shortages in at least one fuel type despite overall EU reserves meeting minimum requirements
Romania faces the most severe crisis with petrol reserves at only 43.6 days, less than half the mandatory 90-day minimum
Major economies including Germany (52.7 days petrol), France (64.7 days petrol), and Italy (69.5 days petrol) show critical petrol shortages
Jet fuel reserves are particularly tight with 19 countries below the 90-day threshold, averaging just 91.1 days EU-wide
Brent crude declined 10.77% to $88.69 per barrel, offering some price relief though levels remain elevated from recent geopolitical tensions
